data.title: Toy Association unveils 2026 toy trends at 120th Toy Fair®, including "Forever Young" and "Express Yourself".
data.description: 2026 is set to be shaped by key trends including "Forever Young," "Cozy Culture," "Inspiring Inventors," and "Express Yourself." Recently, on Valentine's Day, The Toy Association™ revealed the upcoming toy and play trends at its 120th Toy Fair® in New York. Celebrating the essence of play, this event highlighted the latest trends that are expected to dominate the industry.

data.title: Belo Sun Mining gets Installation License reinstated for Volta Grande Gold Project in Brazil.
data.description: On February 14, 2026, Belo Sun Mining Corp. revealed that the Federal Regional Court has granted acceptance to their appeal to revive the Installation License for the Volta Grande Gold Project situated in Pará State, Brazil. This license had been revoked in 2017. Belo Sun, trading as TSX: BSX and OTCQB: BSXGF, expressed delight over this development.
Ongwe Minerals finalizes reverse takeover and financing, trading to begin soon.

data.title: Physical AI stocks are on the rise as robots and autonomous vehicles expand globally, with Bank of America estimating a trillion-dollar transformation already in progress. Robots, autonomous vehicles, and drones are beginning to scale and operate in the real world, moving intelligence from screens to machines. Nvidia CEO predicts the next wave of AI is physical AI, embedded in the physical world, with systems capable of perceiving environments, reasoning, and executing actions. Robotaxis are already in operation in various cities, and humanoid robots are becoming prominent.
data.description: The article discusses the shift in AI technology from being predominantly screen-based to physical machines that can interact in the real world. This new phase, termed as Physical AI, includes robots assembling cars, self-driving cars, and drones for deliveries. Bank of America's thematic investing team estimates this transformation to be worth trillions of dollars, with value transitioning from digital models to physical machines. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang emphasizes that machines are beginning to understand, reason, and act in reality, marking the era of Physical AI. The article highlights the difference between the previous AI wave, focusing on content creation, and the new wave, embedding intelligence in physical systems like robots and autonomous vehicles. Robotaxis are already operational in several cities, and advanced driver-assistance systems are expected to achieve mass adoption in China by 2030. The decreasing hardware costs and advancements in onboard computing are set to revolutionize mobility by reducing costs in ride-hailing and freight industries. The rise of humanoid robots is seen as a significant aspect of this technological transformation.

data.title: NowVertical consolidates under one brand, surpasses integration target, enhancing revenue growth for 2026.
data.description: The successful implementation of the One Brand strategy has led to improved operating efficiency and enhanced cross-selling capabilities, positioning the company for scalable growth in 2026. The strategy has resulted in a significant increase in operating leverage and has fortified the company's platform for future expansion. This achievement has set the stage for continued success and development, with promising prospects for the company's growth in the upcoming year.

data.title: Trump believes Kevin Warsh could drive over 15% economic growth, regrets not appointing him as Fed chair; Senate confirmation may be delayed due to probe.
data.description: President Donald Trump showed support for his Federal Reserve chairman nominee, Kevin Warsh, believing he could lead the U.S. economy to new heights. During an interview with Fox Business, Trump mentioned that with Warsh's capabilities, the economy could potentially grow at a rate of 15% or even more. Trump also admitted that not selecting Warsh for the Federal Reserve chair in 2017 was a significant error. However, there might be a delay in Warsh's Senate confirmation as Republican Senator Thom Tillis has pledged to hold off on all Federal Reserve nominations while the Trump administration conducts a Justice Department investigation into Powell. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: ReelTime significantly reduces potential dilution by 74% with capital reset, boosting per-share value. Tech giants increase share counts for AI funding.
data.description: While major tech companies such as NVIDIA (NVDA), Alphabet (GOOGL), Palantir (PLTR), Microsoft (MSFT), and Meta Platforms (META) are increasing their share counts to finance capital-intensive AI projects, ReelTime Media (RLTR) is taking a different approach by enhancing the value per share. The mentioned tech giants are expanding their share counts to fund AI ventures, whereas RLTR is opting to boost per-share value.

data.title: Software stocks drop for an eighth day, AI concerns weigh; Bitcoin at 16-month low, $66k; iShares Tech-Software ETF down 30%.
data.description: Software stocks experienced a steep decline for an eight consecutive session on Thursday, indicating the sector's longest losing streak since May 2021, amid concerns that artificial intelligence may disrupt demand in the SaaS market. Ralliant shares are close to critical levels, facing pressure due to various factors. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSE: IGV) dropped 1.9%, marking a 30% decline from late-October highs. Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), synonymous with the AI-driven software surge, fell 4.4% following an 11% drop the previous day. The most significant software losers were linked to the crypto market, as Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) fell by 9% to $66,000, levels last witnessed in October 2024. Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ: MSTR), Mara Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: MARA), and CleanSpark Inc. (NASDAQ: CLSK) each witnessed around a 12% decrease. In parallel, commodities faced widespread decreases, with silver also experiencing a significant plunge. This information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: US stock futures rise after sell-off. Trump launches drug price website. January jobs report delayed to next week. Dow +0.23%, S&P +0.40%, Nasdaq +0.52%. Amazon drops 8.39%, Reddit up 11.22%. Fed likely to keep March rates unchanged. Treasury bond yields: 10-year 4.20%, 2-year 3.48%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Friday after a previous sell-off, with major benchmark indices higher. President Trump announced a new website to reduce drug prices, offering affordable medications. The January jobs report was delayed due to the recent government shutdown. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.20%, two-year bond at 3.48%. Market projections showed an 81.3% likelihood of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in March. Index performance showed Dow Jones +0.23%, S&P 500 +0.40%, Nasdaq 100 +0.52%, Russell 2000 +0.95%. SPY and QQQ ETFs were higher in premarket, with SPY at $680.91 (+0.49%) and QQQ at $600.83 (+0.64%). Amazon (AMZN) dropped 8.39% in premarket due to mixed Q4 financial results, while Reddit (RDDT) jumped 11.22% on strong Q4 performance.

data.title: Stock futures decline after tech selloff with Dow down 0.32%, S&P 500 down 0.54%, Nasdaq 100 down 0.80%. Jobless claims stable at 209,000. Treasury bonds at 10-year yield of 4.27%. Federal Reserve likely to keep rates unchanged in March. Alphabet (GOOG) drops 1.68% despite strong Q4 results. SPY down 0.49% at $682.83, QQQ down 0.81% at $601.82.
data.description: On Thursday, U.S. stock futures declined after finishing mixed the day before, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping 351 points. Amazon.com Inc. is set to report earnings. U.S. jobless claims for the week ending Jan. 31 were stable at 209,000, slightly lower than expected. 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.27% and two-year bond at 3.55%. Markets project a 90.1% chance of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March. Indices performance showed Dow Jones -0.32%, S&P 500 -0.54%, Nasdaq 100 -0.80%, and Russell 2000 -0.47%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) was down 0.49% at $682.83, Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) declined 0.81% to $601.82. Alphabet Inc. dropped 1.68% despite strong fourth-quarter results. (Characters: 819)

data.title: Fed's Lisa Cook is concerned about inflation being too high at 2.9%. Also warns of 'K-shaped' economy leaving low-income groups behind. Fed maintains a cautious approach to monetary policy.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook emphasized a cautious approach to monetary policy, describing current interest rates as mildly restrictive. She raised concerns over the economic impact on low-income families due to a "K-shaped" economic divergence. Cook supported the FOMC's decision to maintain the policy rate, noting stagnation in progress towards inflation targets. PCE inflation stood at 2.9%, above the Fed's 2% target, with core goods prices increasing notably due to tariff hikes. Cook stressed the need for strong evidence of sustainable inflation decline to focus on preventing entrenched higher inflation. She spoke at the Economic Club of Miami and the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump admits passing over Kevin Warsh for Fed chair in 2017 was a "really big mistake" due to pressure from Treasury Secretary. Plans to nominate Warsh for Fed chair in 2026, aiming for 15% economic growth. Read more on Benzinga.com.
data.description: President Donald Trump admitted to making a significant error in overlooking Kevin Warsh for Federal Reserve Chair in 2017, instead choosing Jerome Powell. Trump attributed this oversight to pressure from his then-Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin. The President described Warsh as the "runner-up" for the position and expressed regret for not selecting him, calling it a "really big mistake." Trump has now nominated Warsh to succeed Powell when his term expires in May 2026, citing confidence in Warsh's ability to drive U.S. economic growth to 15%. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Compliance exec: Hong Kong, Thailand, Marshall Islands exploring digital distribution of social benefits.
data.description: Hong Kong, Thailand, and the Marshall Islands are investigating the use of tokenized debt instruments and managing social benefit programs through blockchain technology. This innovative approach aims to increase efficiency and transparency in financial operations.

data.title: Founders of Melissa & Doug brand, Doug and Melissa Bernstein, inducted into Toy Hall of Fame at Toy of the Year Awards.
data.description: In Wilton, Connecticut, on February 14, 2026, Doug and Melissa Bernstein, the creators of the value-based children's brand Melissa & Doug®, were honored by being inducted into the Toy Industry Hall of Fame during the 2026 Toy Foundation's Toy of the Year® Awards Gala held in New York City. This prestigious recognition highlights their contributions to the industry.

data.title: DHS issues subpoenas to tech firms to identify protest accounts against ICE. Companies comply under pressure.
data.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is issuing numerous administrative subpoenas to social media companies to uncover accounts involved in protesting against Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). While facing resistance, social media firms are mainly cooperating with the requests. Tech reporter Sheera Frenkel provides insights into this situation.
Fed's Goolsbee: I don't know how restrictive Fed policy is. I still think it would've been wiser in December to wait.

data.title: Real estate mogul Grant Cardone is selling his 10,000 sq ft mansion for 700 BTC ($46.58 million), betting on Bitcoin's future.
data.description: Real estate mogul Grant Cardone is offering his 10,000 sq ft Golden Beach mansion for 700 BTC, valued at $46.58 million currently. The mansion includes 7 bedrooms, 6 garages, and a private shoreline. Cardone is targeting early Bitcoin holders to make the move, betting on BTC's long-term appreciation for superior returns. He compared his offer to his neighbor's property listed at $88 million in fiat, suggesting he believes holding BTC will be more profitable in the long run. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Luki Lab introduces six new toy brands, including Spherehedz, Gem Pets, and Melon Mayhem, focusing on original IP. #ToyBrands #Creativity
data.description: A diverse range of products including action figures, collectibles, and games is being introduced to the market, with prominent releases such as Spherehedz, Gem Pets, and Melon Mayhem leading the way. This move highlights the company's dedication to developing original concepts and engaging gameplay. The announcement was made in Irvine, California on February 14, 2026, demonstrating the company's commitment to catering to the demand for innovative toys that encourage creativity among families.

data.title: Hunter Nation launches campaign to repeal Prop 4 in Utah, mobilizing sportsmen and gun owners through digital outreach and petition drive. Featuring support from Donald J. Trump Jr. and Ted Nugent.
data.description: In Salt Lake City on February 14, 2026, Hunter Nation, a prominent hunting advocacy group, initiated a targeted digital outreach, petition initiative, and video promotion featuring Donald J. Trump Jr. and Ted Nugent. This effort was aimed at galvanizing Utah sportsmen and gun owners.
